subject: Geography
overall_learning_objectives: Identify the gases that make up our atmosphere
Understand the importance of the atmosphere for life on Earth
Identify the five layers of the atmosphere
Distinguish between weather and climate in simple terms
Learn about temperature, air pressure, and wind
Understand the basic forms of rainfall
Recognize the impact of global warming and the importance of oxygen

lesson_plan.title: Composition of the Atmosphere
topics_covered: Nitrogen and Oxygen: The main gases
Importance of $CO_2$ for the Green House effect
teaching_strategy: Pie chart demonstration of gas percentages
Discussion: 'What happens if we cut trees?'
student_activities: Draw a colorful pie chart showing the composition of air
List 3 ways trees help us breathe
assessment: Oral quiz: Which gas is most plentiful in the air?
Identify the gas used by plants to make food

lesson_plan.title: Structure of the Atmosphere - Part 1
topics_covered: Troposphere: The layer we breathe in
Stratosphere: The layer for airplanes and Ozone
teaching_strategy: Visual aids showing clouds in the Troposphere
student_activities: Sketch the first two layers of the atmosphere
Identify which layer protects us from harmful sun rays
assessment: Short answer: Why is the Stratosphere good for flying planes?

lesson_plan.title: Structure of the Atmosphere - Part 2
teaching_strategy: Storytelling: 'The journey of a shooting star'
Discussion on how we hear the radio/mobile signals
student_activities: Complete the full diagram of the 5 layers
Label the layer where space shuttles orbit
assessment: Naming the 5 layers in order from bottom to top

lesson_plan.title: Weather and Climate
teaching_strategy: Comparing today's weather with the city's general climate
Explaining why it is hotter at the equator
student_activities: Record the weather for the day (Sunny/Cloudy/Rainy)
Draw a thermometer and mark the boiling point of water

lesson_plan.title: Air Pressure and Wind
topics_covered: How air moves: High pressure to Low pressure
Types of Winds: Permanent, Seasonal, and Local
teaching_strategy: Balloon experiment to show air has weight
student_activities: Identify 'Loo' as a local wind of India
Match wind types to examples (e.g., Monsoon - Seasonal)
assessment: Short answer: How does air pressure change with height?

lesson_plan.title: Moisture and Rainfall
topics_covered: Three types of rainfall: Convectional, Orographic, and Cyclonic
teaching_strategy: Diagrams of the 3 types of rainfall
Using a glass of ice-water to show condensation
student_activities: Draw the three types of rainfall
Write 2 lines on why rain is important for plants
assessment: Identifying a rainfall type from a drawing
Naming the instrument used to measure rainfall

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Summary
student_activities: Complete the summary table in the notebook

teaching_aids: Charts showing layers of the atmosphere
Pictures of different wind vanes and rain gauges
teacher_tip: To explain air pressure easily, tell students that air is like a pile of blankets—the one at the bottom (us) feels the most weight/pressure, and as you go up, the weight decreases!
